backlog.title: QA Bug Fixes (rev 002 findings)
backlog.description: Fix six issues found during end-to-end QA of the tmux-livepicker rev 002 implementation: 3 Major (index corruption, window-mode duplicate link, create-on-confirm orphan) and 3 Minor (TOCTOU race, sentinel resolution, detached resize). All fixes preserve the 'full, exact restoration' invariant (PRD §9/§15) and the supersede guarantee (PRD §18).

milestones.title: Major Issues: State Corruption & User Stranding
milestones.description: Fix the three Major issues that silently corrupt session/window state or strand the user. Issue 1 fires on the default code path for any user whose active window is not the last window.

tasks.title: Fix link-window -a index shift (Issue 1)
tasks.description: The -a flag inserts the linked preview window AFTER the active window, shifting subsequent indices when the active window is not the last. Remove -a so links append at the end, preserving original indices. Also strengthen existing tests to assert on window indices, not just IDs.

subtasks.title: Remove -a flag from link-window call in preview.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_facc52335e68/bugfix/001_c7c6203f3e28/architecture/issue1_2_findings.md §Issue 1. The `-a` flag means 'insert AFTER active window'. Without -a, link-window appends at the next free index at the END. Empirically verified: `link-window -s @id -t session:` appends at end and unlinks cleanly back to original indices.
2. INPUT: scripts/preview.sh line 187 contains `if ! tmux link-window -a -s "$src_id" -t "$current_session:" 2>/dev/null; then`. The `-a` flag is the sole root cause.
3. LOGIC: Remove the `-a` flag from that single `link-window` call. Change to `tmux link-window -s "$src_id" -t "$current_session:"`. Update the inline comment block above the call (lines ~150-154 referencing 'link-window -a') to explain why bare link-window (append at end) is correct and -a (insert after active) was the bug. Also update the file header comment at line 1-2 if it references §13's `-a` prescription. The PRD §13 prescribes -a but this bugfix overrides that prescription; note the deviation in the comment.
4. OUTPUT: preview.sh's link-window call no longer uses -a. The linked preview window always appends at the end of the driver session, so no existing window index shifts.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline comment at the link-window call site in preview.sh to document the deviation from PRD §13's -a prescription and why bare link-window preserves indices. No README/CHANGELOG change in this subtask (handled by final sync task P1.M3.T1).

subtasks.title: Add window-index assertion tests for multi-window driver
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue1_2_findings.md §Test Coverage Gap. The shipped tests assert on window IDs (#{window_id}) not indices (#{window_index}). Window IDs are unchanged by an index shift, so the tests pass even when indices are corrupted. The baseline fixture (tests/setup_socket.sh) creates driver with an 'extra' window + pane splits.
2. INPUT: The driver session (TEST_DRIVER_SESSION) has at least 2 windows from setup_socket.sh (the initial window + the 'extra' window). preview.sh links candidate windows into this driver for live preview.
3. LOGIC: Add two new test functions:
   (a) test_preview_preserves_window_indices_cancel: Create a driver with 3 windows (add a 3rd via `tmux new-window -t driver`). Select the FIRST window (not last). Snapshot `tmux list-windows -t '=driver' -F '#{window_index}:#{window_name}'` BEFORE activation. Run livepicker.sh activate, input-handler.sh next-session (links a preview), input-handler.sh cancel. Snapshot the same AFTER cancel. Assert byte-equality via assert_eq.
   (b) test_preview_preserves_window_indices_confirm: Same setup, but instead of cancel, navigate to a foreign session and confirm. Assert the driver's window indices are unchanged after confirm (the driver is left corrupted in both paths per the bug report).
   Place these in tests/test_preview.sh (follows the existing pattern of lp_preview_seed_state + preview.sh calls) OR tests/test_restore.sh (follows the activate->nav->cancel pattern with attach_test_client). Use attach_test_client because the full activate flow needs a client. Pin @livepicker-preview-defer off (setup_test does this).
4. OUTPUT: Two new test_* functions that would have CAUGHT Issue 1 by asserting on window indices. The test suite (tests/run.sh) auto-discovers test_* functions.
5. MOCKING: No external services. Uses the isolated socket harness (tests/setup_socket.sh). Mock nothing — tests drive the real scripts against the real isolated tmux server.
6. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. Test-only.

tasks.title: Fix window-mode self-session duplicate link (Issue 2)
tasks.description: In window mode, the self-session guard compares $S (a session:index token) to the bare session name, which never matches for the driver's own windows. Extend the guard to extract the session from the token.

subtasks.title: Extend self-session guard in preview.sh for window mode
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue1_2_findings.md §Issue 2. The self-session guard at preview.sh line ~126 checks `[ "$S" = "$current_session" ]`. In window mode, $S is a `session:index` token (e.g. `driver:1`), which never equals the bare session name. `${S%%:*}` correctly extracts the session name from the token.
2. INPUT: preview.sh line ~126, the self-session guard block. $S is either a bare session name (session mode) or a `session:index` token (window mode). $current_session is always the bare driver session name (from @livepicker-orig-session).
3. LOGIC: Before the guard comparison, compute the session to compare:
   ```
   local check_session="$S"
   if [ "$(opt_type)" = "window" ] && [ "${S%%:*}" != "$S" ]; then
       check_session="${S%%:*}"
   fi
   ```
   Then change `[ "$S" = "$current_session" ]` to `[ "$check_session" = "$current_session" ]`. When the guard fires in window mode (driver-owned window), the self-session path must SELECT the specific window, not just ORIG_WINDOW. In the self-session block (currently lines ~126-138), when opt_type==window, resolve the src_id of $S's window and `tmux select-window -t "$src_id"` instead of (or in addition to) select-window ORIG_WINDOW. The src_id resolution code already exists later in the function (lines ~140-150 for window mode); it should be moved or duplicated before the self-session guard so the guard can select the correct window. OR: simpler approach — when opt_type==window and check_session==current_session, just `select-window -t "$S"` (the session:index token works directly with select-window in the driver session).
4. OUTPUT: preview.sh's self-session guard fires for driver-owned windows in window mode. No duplicate link is created. The correct window is selected.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline comment at the self-session guard block to document the window-mode extension (session extraction from token). No README/CHANGELOG change here.

subtasks.title: Add window-mode driver-owned-window preview test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue1_2_findings.md §Required New Test. The shipped window-mode tests use targets in OTHER sessions (alpha/beta). None preview a window that lives in the driver session.
2. INPUT: The driver session (TEST_DRIVER_SESSION) has at least 2 windows from setup_socket.sh. preview.sh in window mode is called with a `session:index` token.
3. LOGIC: Add test_window_preview_driver_self_no_duplicate to tests/test_preview.sh:
   - Set @livepicker-type window
   - lp_preview_seed_state (sets @livepicker-orig-session, @livepicker-orig-window)
   - Call preview.sh with a DRIVER-OWNED window token: e.g. `preview.sh "$TEST_DRIVER_SESSION:1"` (the driver's window at index 1)
   - Assert @livepicker-linked-id is EMPTY (no link was attempted — the self-session guard fired)
   - Assert the driver's window list has NO duplicate @id entries (count windows, verify unique ids)
   - Assert the correct window was selected (the active window id matches the token's window)
   Follow the pattern of test_self_session_no_link (which tests the session-mode equivalent).
4. OUTPUT: One new test_* function that catches Issue 2 by verifying no duplicate window is created when previewing a driver-owned window in window mode.
5. MOCKING: No external services. Uses the isolated socket harness.
6. DOCS: none — test-only.

tasks.title: Fix create-on-confirm name sanitization (Issue 3)
tasks.description: The create gate checks has-session with the raw query, but tmux sanitizes '.' and ':' to '_', so the gate fails on valid names, orphaning the created session. Replace the gate with a -P -F '#{session_name}' capture of the actual created name.

subtasks.title: Replace has-session gate with -P -F name capture in input-handler.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue3_findings.md. The confirm branch at input-handler.sh line ~401 runs `tmux new-session ... && tmux has-session -t "=$query"`. tmux sanitizes '.' and ':' to '_', so has-session checks the WRONG name. The fix: use `new-session -P -F '#{session_name}'` to capture the actual created name, then switch to THAT name. Empirically verified: `new-session -P -F '#{session_name}' -d -s 'my.proj'` outputs `my_proj`.
2. INPUT: input-handler.sh, the confirm branch's create-on-no-match path (lines ~379-405). The variables in scope: $query (the raw typed filter), $new_session_args (the new-session argv array, currently `(-d -s "$query")` optionally with zoxide `-c -n`), and _confirm_land_on_session (the switch+restore helper).
3. LOGIC: Modify the create block:
   (a) Add `-P -F '#{session_name}'` to new_session_args: change `local new_session_args=(-d -s "$query")` to `local new_session_args=(-d -P -F '#{session_name}' -s "$query")`.
   (b) Replace the gate: change from `if tmux new-session ... && tmux has-session -t "=$query"; then _confirm_land_on_session "$query"` to `local created=""; created="$(tmux new-session "${new_session_args[@]}" 2>/dev/null)"; if [ -n "$created" ]; then _confirm_land_on_session "$created"`.
   (c) Keep the else branch (restore.sh cancel) — it now fires only when creation genuinely failed (empty query, collision, etc.).
   (d) Update the comment block (lines ~392-400) that documents the gate's rationale — the has-session gate was the bug; the -P capture is the fix.
4. OUTPUT: input-handler.sh's create path captures the actual created name and switches to it. No orphan session is left behind. Empty query or collision → created="" → cancel (correct).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline comment at the create block to document that the gate now captures the actual name via -P instead of checking the raw query. No README/CHANGELOG change here.

subtasks.title: Add sanitized-name create test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue3_findings.md §Test Coverage Gap. All three existing create tests use pure-alphanumeric queries that are NEVER sanitized. The sanitization path is untested.
2. INPUT: tests/test_create.sh, the test file with test_create_on_creates_and_activates, test_create_off_creates_nothing, test_window_mode_creates_nothing. Uses attach_test_client + livepicker.sh activate + input-handler.sh type/confirm.
3. LOGIC: Add test_create_sanitized_name_lands_on_session to tests/test_create.sh:
   - Set @livepicker-create on
   - Type a query containing '.' (e.g. `my.proj`)
   - Confirm
   - Assert the session was created under the SANITIZED name (has-session -t '=my_proj' succeeds)
   - Assert the raw name does NOT exist (has-session -t '=my.proj' fails — or just skip this assertion since it's redundant)
   - Assert the client landed on the sanitized name (display-message -p '#{session_name}' == 'my_proj')
   - Assert no orphan sessions exist (the total session count = original + 1, not + 2)
   Follow the pattern of test_create_on_creates_and_activates exactly.
4. OUTPUT: One new test_* function that catches Issue 3 by typing a dotted query and asserting the user lands on the sanitized session.
5. MOCKING: No external services.
6. DOCS: none — test-only.

milestones.title: Minor Issues: Race, Rendering, Limitations
milestones.description: Fix the three Minor issues: the TOCTOU race in the deferred-preview supersede guard (Issue 4), the sentinel resolution gap for session-context format specifiers (Issue 5), and document the inherent detached-candidate resize side effect (Issue 6).

tasks.title: Close TOCTOU race in deferred-preview supersede guard (Issue 4)
tasks.description: Add a third seq check immediately before set_state LINKED_ID (after select-window) to prevent a stale deferred job from committing an orphaned link. Also add an idempotent pre-link check to prevent duplicate links from interleaved jobs.

subtasks.title: Add third seq check + idempotent pre-link check in preview.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue4_5_6_findings.md §Issue 4. The TOCTOU window is between GUARD 2 (preview.sh line ~173) and set_state LINKED_ID (line ~198), spanned by three tmux round-trips (unlink, link, select). The fix has two parts:
   A. GUARD 3: Insert a third seq check between select-window (line ~195) and set_state LINKED_ID (line ~198):
      ```
      if [ -n "$expected_seq" ]; then
          [ "$(get_state "$STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ" "0")" != "$expected_seq" ] && return 0
      fi
      ```
      This closes the commit-clobber half: a job that went stale during the unlink→link→select round-trips will NOT overwrite the newer job's STATE_LINKED_ID.
   B. IDEMPOTENT PRE-LINK CHECK: Before unlinking+linking (near the existing duplicate-guard at line ~134-138), probe whether src_id is already in the driver:
      ```
      if tmux list-windows -t "=$current_session" -F '#{window_id}' 2>/dev/null | grep -Fxq "$src_id"; then
          tmux select-window -t "$src_id" 2>/dev/null || true
          set_state "$STATE_LINKED_ID" "$src_id"
          return 0
      fi
      ```
      This prevents a losing interleave from creating a duplicate link.
2. INPUT: preview.sh, the mutation block of preview_main (lines ~134-198). The variables $expected_seq, $src_id, $current_session, $STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ, $STATE_LINKED_ID are all in scope. The existing GUARD 1 (lines ~100-103) and GUARD 2 (lines ~172-174) provide the pattern to mirror.
3. LOGIC: Insert GUARD 3 (part A) between select-window and set_state. Insert the idempotent check (part B) before the existing duplicate-guard block or fused with it. Both must be inside the `mode == live` code path (after the preview-mode gate and self-session guard). The GUARD 3 must be placed AFTER select-window but BEFORE set_state — do NOT move it earlier (placing it before link-window would let a stale job link then bail, leaving an untracked link).
4. OUTPUT: preview.sh's mutation block has three supersede guards (entry, pre-mutation, pre-commit) and an idempotent pre-link check. A stale deferred job that passes GUARD 2 but is superseded during the round-trips is caught by GUARD 3 before committing. A duplicate link from interleaved jobs is prevented by the existence probe.
5. MOCKING: The seq check reads @livepicker-preview-seq via get_state — no external services to mock.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline comments at the GUARD 3 insertion point and the idempotent check to document the race closure. Reference PRD §18 supersede guarantee and issue4_5_6_findings.md.

tasks.title: Fix sentinel resolution for session-context specifiers (Issue 5)
tasks.description: The sentinel session name is unique per activation (PID+epoch), so session-state specifiers like #S/#{session_name} resolve to a non-swappable string. Use a stable sentinel session name (__lp_sentinel__) and add a second placeholder swap in the renderer.

subtasks.title: Use stable sentinel session name + second renderer swap
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue4_5_6_findings.md §Issue 5. Empirically verified: `#W` against sentinel resolves to `__lp_tab__` (swappable). `#{session_name}` against sentinel resolves to `__lp_sent_123_456` (NOT swappable). The fix: make the sentinel session name a fixed placeholder `__lp_sentinel__` and swap BOTH `__lp_tab__` and `__lp_sentinel__` in the renderer.
2. INPUT: Two files need changes:
   (a) scripts/livepicker.sh, `_lp_resolve_tab_templates()` line ~76: `sent_sess="__lp_sent_$$_$(date +%s)"` — the unique sentinel session name.
   (b) scripts/renderer.sh, window-status render path line ~130: `ws_seg="${ws_tpl//__lp_tab__/$esc_wname}"` — the sole placeholder swap.
3. LOGIC:
   (a) In livepicker.sh, BEFORE creating the sentinel session, add `tmux kill-session -t "__lp_sentinel__" 2>/dev/null || true` to clean any stray sentinel from a crashed prior run. Then change `sent_sess="__lp_sent_$$_$(date +%s)"` to `sent_sess="__lp_sentinel__"`.
   (b) In renderer.sh, after the existing `__lp_tab__` swap (line ~130), add a second swap: `ws_seg="${ws_seg//__lp_sentinel__/$esc_wname}"`. The `${var//pat/rep}` expansion does NOT re-scan the replacement, so no recursion risk (the existing comment at renderer.sh lines ~128-129 documents this).
   (c) In livepicker.sh, the unexpanded-'#{` guard (lines ~102-103) already catches malformed themes. The sentinel-name swap is orthogonal — if the theme has BOTH #W and #S, both placeholders get swapped.
4. OUTPUT: When a theme's window-status-format contains `#{session_name}` or `#S`, the sentinel resolution bakes `__lp_sentinel__` into the template (instead of `__lp_sent_PID_EPOCH`), and the renderer swaps it to the actual session name. Tabs render correctly for themes that use session-state specifiers.
5. MOCKING: No external services. The sentinel session is created and destroyed within _lp_resolve_tab_templates on the live tmux server.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline comments at livepicker.sh line ~76 (sentinel session name) and renderer.sh line ~130 (second swap) to document the session-name placeholder. Reference PRD §17 and issue4_5_6_findings.md.

tasks.title: Document detached candidate resize limitation (Issue 6)
tasks.description: Linking a detached candidate window into an attached driver resizes the shared window object to the driver's dimensions. On unlink, the candidate's size is NOT restored. Document this as a known limitation in README.md.

subtasks.title: Add Known Limitations section to README.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See issue4_5_6_findings.md §Issue 6. The README has NO 'Known Limitations' section. The 'How it works' section ends at ~line 164 (after 'Cancelling leaves zero trace.'). The next section is '## Compatibility' at ~line 166. tmux's window-size auto resizes a linked window to the largest attached client across ALL sessions.
2. INPUT: README.md, the region between 'How it works' (ends ~line 164) and '## Compatibility' (~line 166).
3. LOGIC: Insert a new subsection after 'How it works':
   ```markdown
   ### Known limitations

   - **Detached candidate windows are resized during preview.** When a detached
     candidate window is linked into the attached driver session for live
     preview, tmux resizes the shared window object to the driver's dimensions.
     After the picker exits, the candidate's window retains the driver's size
     rather than its original dimensions. This is inherent to tmux's
     `window-size` behavior and affects only detached candidate sessions. To
     avoid this, set `@livepicker-preview-mode snapshot` (uses `capture-pane`
     and never links the window).
   ```
   Place this between the end of 'How it works' and '## Compatibility'.
4. OUTPUT: README.md has a 'Known Limitations' subsection documenting the detached-candidate resize behavior and the snapshot workaround.
5. MOCKING: No external services. README.md is a static document.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] This subtask IS the documentation change — it adds the Known Limitations content to README.md directly.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
milestones.description: Final documentation sweep: update CHANGELOG.md with all six bug fix entries and update README.md overview/feature sections that summarize the changeset. This task runs LAST and depends on every implementing subtask.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Update CHANGELOG.md with entries for all six bug fixes. Verify README.md feature sections are consistent with the code changes (e.g., PRD §13 -a deviation, create path behavior).

subtasks.title: Update CHANGELOG.md with bug fix entries
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_facc52335e68/bugfix/001_c7c6203f3e28/architecture/system_context.md for the full changeset summary. CHANGELOG.md already has entries for prior bugfix rounds (see git log: 'Document five adversarial QA bugfixes in changelog'). Follow the existing CHANGELOG entry format.
2. INPUT: All six bug fixes are implemented (all dependencies satisfied). The changeset is: Issue 1 (link-window -a removal), Issue 2 (window-mode self-session guard), Issue 3 (create name capture), Issue 4 (TOCTOU third seq check + idempotent link), Issue 5 (sentinel session-name swap), Issue 6 (README known limitation).
3. LOGIC: Add a new CHANGELOG.md section (or entries) documenting all six fixes. Follow the existing format (the CHANGELOG has prior 'Fix' entries with severity, PRD reference, and description). Group by severity: 3 Major fixes first, then 3 Minor. Each entry should reference the PRD section and the symptom. Example:
   ```
   ### Fixed
   - **Window index corruption on preview** (Major): `link-window -a` shifted
     the driver's window indices; now appends at the end. (PRD §9/§15)
   - **Window-mode duplicate link for driver windows** (Major): self-session
     guard now handles `session:index` tokens. (PRD §7)
   - **Create-on-confirm orphan session** (Major): now captures the actual
     created name via `new-session -P`. (PRD §6)
   - **Deferred-preview TOCTOU race** (Minor): added third supersede check
     and idempotent pre-link probe. (PRD §18)
   - **Sentinel tab rendering with session-state specifiers** (Minor):
     sentinel session name is now a swappable placeholder. (PRD §17)
   - **Detached candidate resize** (Minor): documented as known limitation.
     (PRD §7/§15)
   ```
4. OUTPUT: CHANGELOG.md has entries for all six fixes, consistent with the existing format.
5. MOCKING: No external services. CHANGELOG.md is a static document.
6. DOCS: [Mode B] This subtask IS the changeset-level documentation sync. It sweeps all six fixes into the CHANGELOG.

subtasks.title: Verify and update README.md feature/behavior sections
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The README.md 'How it works' section (lines ~157-164) describes the link-window + select-window mechanism. The PRD §13 prescribes `link-window -a` but the fix removes `-a`. The README should not reference `-a` (verify it doesn't). The create behavior section (if any) should reflect that names are sanitized. The 'Known limitations' section added by P1.M2.T3.S1 should be consistent with surrounding content.
2. INPUT: README.md (209 lines). The changeset touches: link-window behavior (no -a), create path (sanitized names land correctly), sentinel tab rendering (session-name support), and known limitations (detached resize).
3. LOGIC: Read README.md and verify:
   (a) No reference to `link-window -a` (the fix removed -a). If any mention exists, update it to describe appending at the end.
   (b) The create behavior description (if present) is consistent with the sanitized-name fix (user lands on the created session even with dots in the name).
   (c) The window-status tab section (if present) does not claim `#S`/`#{session_name}` is unsupported (the fix adds support).
   (d) The 'Known limitations' section (from P1.M2.T3.S1) is properly placed and formatted.
   (e) Any 'Features' or 'Behavior' overview sections are consistent with the code changes.
   Make minimal edits — only update sections that are now inconsistent. If everything is already consistent (no references to -a, create, or sentinel limitations), note that no changes were needed.
4. OUTPUT: README.md is consistent with all code changes from this bugfix cycle. No stale references to removed behavior.
5. MOCKING: No external services.
6. DOCS: [Mode B] This subtask is the changeset-level README sweep. It catches any overview/feature section that summarizes behavior changed by the bugfix.
